info.title: Recipe API
info.description:
B2B Recipe API providing structured recipes with comprehensive nutrition data.

**Quick Start** - Try it now (no API key needed):
```
curl https://recipe-api.com/api/v1/dinner
```

**Sample Responses** - All endpoints below show example responses so you can evaluate the data structure before signing up. Expand any endpoint to see what you'll receive.

**Authentication** - Protected endpoints require an `X-API-Key` header. Get your key at [recipe-api.com](https://recipe-api.com/#pricing).

tags.name: Public
tags.description: No authentication required. Try `/api/v1/dinner` for a complete recipe example.

tags.name: Discovery
tags.description: Requires API key. No credit cost. Browse categories, cuisines, and dietary options.

tags.name: Recipes
tags.description: Browse recipes free. Full recipe detail costs 1 credit. Sample data shown inline.

get.description: Returns a single delicious dinner recipe with all fields. Perfect for testing the API schema. Try it: `curl https://recipe-api.com/api/v1/dinner`

example.name: Texas Chili con Carne
example.description: A thick, beef-based stew featuring tender cubes of meat in a rich sauce made from reconstituted whole chilies and aromatic spices without beans or tomatoes.

freezer.notes: Thaw overnight in refrigerator before reheating.

storage.reheating: Heat in a saucepan over medium-low heat, adding a splash of water if too thick.

equipment.name: Blender
equipment.alternative: Food processor or mortar and pestle

instructions.text: Tear the dried chilies into strips and place them in a bowl. Cover with 240ml of boiling water and soak for 30 minutes.
Heat olive oil in a heavy skillet over medium-high heat. Brown the beef cubes on all sides until a crust forms.

doneness_cues.visual: Beef is deeply browned on all sides

instructions.text: Reduce heat to low, cover, and simmer for 1 hour until beef is fork-tender.

troubleshooting.likely_cause: The meat has not simmered long enough.
troubleshooting.prevention: Ensure liquid is at a very low simmer with lid sealed.
troubleshooting.fix: Continue simmering in 15-minute increments until tender.

chef_notes: For deeper flavor, use a variety of dried chilies like ancho, guajillo, and pasilla.
example.cultural_context: Texas Chili is a hearty stew deeply rooted in Texan culinary tradition, distinguished by the absence of beans and tomatoes.

Returns all ingredient categories with counts. No credit cost.
Returns a paginated list of recipes with optional filtering. Discovery limited to 500 recipes; use filters for larger catalogs. No credit cost.

parameters.name: ingredients
parameters.description: Comma-separated ingredient UUIDs (returns recipes containing ALL specified ingredients)

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Page number (discovery limited to 500 total recipes)

total.description: Total matching recipes (capped at 500 for discovery)

total_capped.description: True if more recipes exist beyond the 500-recipe discovery limit

get.description: Returns complete recipe details including ingredients, instructions, and nutrition. **Costs 1 credit.**

200.description: Full recipe details with usage info

data.name: Texas Chili con Carne
data.description: A thick, beef-based stew featuring tender cubes of meat in a rich sauce made from reconstituted whole chilies and aromatic spices without beans or tomatoes.

freezer.notes: Thaw overnight in refrigerator before reheating.

storage.reheating: Heat in a saucepan over medium-low heat, adding a splash of water if too thick.

equipment.name: Blender
equipment.alternative: Food processor or mortar and pestle

instructions.text: Tear the dried chilies into strips and place them in a bowl. Cover with 240ml (1 cup) of boiling water and soak for 30 minutes.
Drain the chilies and set them aside. Reserve the soaking liquid for the cooking process.
Heat olive oil in a heavy skillet over medium-high heat. Brown the beef cubes on all sides until a crust forms.

doneness_cues.visual: Beef is deeply browned on all sides

instructions.text: Pour the reserved chili soaking liquid into the skillet. Add bay leaves and bring the liquid to a boil.
Reduce the heat to low, cover the skillet, and simmer for 1 hour.
Combine the soaked chilies, garlic, cumin, oregano, paprika, sugar, salt, and pepper in a blender. Puree until smooth, adding a small amount of fresh water if the mixture is too thick.
Stir the chili puree into the skillet with the beef. Cover and continue to simmer for 30 minutes.
Remove and discard the bay leaves. Taste and add more salt or pepper if needed before serving.

troubleshooting.likely_cause: The meat has not simmered long enough to break down connective tissue.
troubleshooting.prevention: Ensure the liquid is at a very low simmer and the lid is tightly sealed.
troubleshooting.fix: Continue simmering in 15-minute increments until tender.

troubleshooting.likely_cause: Too much water added during blending or insufficient reduction.
troubleshooting.prevention: Add water to the blender only 15ml at a time.
troubleshooting.fix: Simmer uncovered for the final 15 minutes to evaporate excess moisture.

chef_notes: For the deepest chili flavor, use a variety of dried chilies like ancho, guajillo, and pasilla. Toasting them briefly before soaking can enhance their aroma.
Don't overcrowd the skillet when browning the beef; sear in batches if necessary to ensure a good crust, which adds significant flavor to the final dish.
The simmering time is crucial for tenderizing the beef. Low and slow is key; the meat should be easily pierced with a fork.

The consistency of the chili can be adjusted by the amount of soaking liquid you use and the final simmering time. If it's too thick, add a splash of water or beef broth; if too thin, simmer uncovered for a bit longer.
data.cultural_context: Texas Chili, or Chili con Carne, is a hearty stew deeply rooted in Texan culinary tradition. Its origins trace back to the mid-19th century, with early versions featuring simple ingredients like dried beef, chilies, and spices, cooked by cowboys and ranchers. The absence of beans and tomatoes is a defining characteristic, distinguishing it from other regional chili variations and emphasizing its pure, meat-and-chili flavor profile.

post.description: Creates a new recipe from structured constraints. Includes USDA-verified nutrition calculation. Requires generate credits (separate from request limits).

parameters.name: dry_run
parameters.description: Return usage and generated content without persisting. Only available when ENABLE_GENERATE_DRY_RUN=true.

phase.description: One of: prep, cook, assemble, finish

Usage.description: API usage information returned with each recipe fetch. Limits vary by plan: Free (100/day), Hobby (500/day), Starter (2,000/day), Pro (50,000/day).

IngredientItem.description: An ingredient from the master ingredients database

source.description: Data source: USDA or Aggregated Public Sources

RecipeResponse.description: Response from generate endpoint containing the created recipe and usage info

error.message: Invalid recipe ID format. Use UUID.
This endpoint requires a paid API key.
Per-minute rate limit exceeded. Please wait before retrying.
Monthly unique recipe limit reached (100). You've accessed 100 different recipes this period. Re-requesting previously accessed recipes doesn't count against this limit. Upgrade for more.
